data.title: Jira bridge outbound LINK/UNLINK sync for relates_to relationships
data.description:
## Context

The Jira bridge outbound path (bridge-outbound.py) processes CREATE, STATUS_CHANGE, and COMMENT events but does not process LINK events. Locally-created relates_to relationships are invisible in Jira. The inbound path already handles Jira issue links correctly. The local ticket system fully supports relates_to (bidirectional LINK events, cycle-safe). Jira Cloud provides a "Relates" link type (symmetric inward/outward "relates to") as a default link type (verified via API: id 10003 on navasage.atlassian.net). This affects any team using DSO with Jira that needs visibility into cross-epic coordination links.

## Success Criteria

- bridge-outbound.py processes LINK events: maps local relates_to to Jira "Relates" link type via name-based lookup (GET /issueLinkType to find "Relates" by name, cache resolved ID; never hardcode link type ID). UNLINK events remove the corresponding Jira link using a read-before-delete pattern (GET issue links, match by both endpoints, DELETE the specific link). Treat 404 on source issue as "already gone" (not error).
- Round-trip verified: create a local relates_to link via CLI, run ticket sync, confirm the link appears in Jira; create a "Relates" link in Jira, run ticket sync, confirm it appears locally
- Jira rejection handled gracefully: writes bridge_alert and persist_relationship_rejection, does not block local link creation or other sync operations
- LINK event sync is idempotent: duplicate events for the same (source, target, relates_to) triple do not create duplicate Jira links — pre-create dedup check queries existing Jira links before creating

## Scenario Analysis

- Jira link type name varies across instances (critical): Name-based lookup addresses this; graceful degradation if "Relates" type not found (bridge_alert, log enumerated types for admin configuration)
- UNLINK removes wrong link (high): Read-before-delete matching both endpoints addresses this
- Duplicate Jira links on retry (high): Pre-create dedup check addresses this
- LINK/UNLINK out-of-order (high): Process events in timestamp order within each sync batch; stale UNLINK for non-existent Jira link writes bridge_alert and continues
- acli API variance across deployment modes (high): Jira Cloud as primary supported mode; Server/DC graceful degradation with bridge_alert

## Dependencies

None — bridge infrastructure work, independent of companion epics. Note: cda8-38b5 (Jira bridge integration tests) may provide test infrastructure relevant to round-trip verification; coordinate on test harness if in-flight.

## Approach

Add LINK/UNLINK event processing to bridge-outbound.py following existing event processing pattern. Name-based link type resolution with cached ID. Read-before-delete for UNLINK safety (treat 404 as "already gone"). Pre-create dedup for idempotency. Timestamp ordering within sync batches. Jira Cloud as primary target; Server/DC with graceful degradation. All Jira failures write bridge_alert without blocking.
